methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA represents intentional knowledge distribution infrastructure in a mid-tier metro, potentially seeding peer-to-peer learning networks that could spread deep AI fluency beyond traditional tech hubs if community engagement scales.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia signals grassroots capacity-building in an historically underserved region, testing whether distributed deep learning can emerge through local institutions rather than platform delivery or metro spillover.

signals.title: NVTC Spotlights Region's AI Trailblazers: Meet the 2026 AI50 - PR Newswire
signals.signal_reading: NVTC's AI50 trailblazer recognition formalizes NoVA's concentration of AI talent and innovation, creating visibility and network effects that further attract capital and expertise to the region while potentially widening the capability gap with other Virginia areas.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA school systems grappling with AI in classrooms signals platform-mediated adoption spreading through education infrastructure, but raises questions about whether students gain deep AI literacy or merely consume AI-enabled tools with shallow understanding.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: AI-powered home search tools in Hampton Roads MLS demonstrate platform-delivered AI reaching non-tech sectors and mid-tier metros, enabling broad but shallow adoption where real estate professionals use AI without necessarily understanding its mechanisms or limitations.
